page_dashboard_subtitle: Overview of your Claude Code configuration
page_projects_subtitle: Manage MCP assignments for your Claude Code projects
page_library_subtitle: All your MCP servers in one place - drag them to projects or global settings
page_profiles_subtitle: Save and switch between different sets of globally-enabled tools
page_subagents_subtitle: Custom sub-agents - drag them to projects or global settings
page_memory_subtitle: Manage CLAUDE.md memory files — persistent instructions loaded at startup
page_marketplace_subtitle: Browse and import Skills and Agents from public repositories
page_insights_subtitle: Session quality, friction analysis, and Claude's insights report
page_analytics_subtitle: Visualize your Claude Code usage patterns
page_skills_subtitle: Agent skills Claude invokes automatically based on context
page_hooks_subtitle: Event-driven automations - run commands or inject prompts on Claude Code events
page_sessions_subtitle: Browse individual Claude Code sessions per project
page_permissions_subtitle: Manage Claude Code permission rules — control what tools Claude can use
page_statusline_subtitle: Customize the real-time info bar at the bottom of your Claude Code terminal
page_comparison_subtitle: Compare usage metrics across projects side-by-side
page_settings_subtitle_models: Configure model defaults, output behavior, and git attribution
page_settings_subtitle_security: Configure bash command isolation, network access, and security settings
page_settings_subtitle_plugins: Manage enabled plugins and custom marketplace sources
page_settings_subtitle_environment: Configure environment variables for Claude Code runtime
page_settings_subtitle_interface: Control visual and interaction preferences for Claude Code
page_settings_subtitle_session: Configure session cleanup, updates, teammate mode, and plans
page_settings_subtitle_authentication: Configure scripts that provide authentication credentials and API keys
page_settings_subtitle_mcp_approval: Control which project-level MCP servers are automatically approved
page_settings_subtitle_keybindings: Customize keyboard shortcuts for Claude Code
page_settings_subtitle_spinner_verbs: Customize the action verbs shown in Claude Code's spinner
page_settings_subtitle_admin: View enterprise managed settings deployed by your IT administrator
page_settings_subtitle_editor_sync: Configure editors, servers, tokens, paths, and backups

placeholder_command_description: Brief description of what this command does
placeholder_command_content_template:
# My Command

This is the prompt content that will be used when the command is invoked.

You can use markdown formatting here.

$ARGUMENTS will be replaced with user input.
placeholder_skill_description: When Claude should invoke this skill
placeholder_skill_content_template:
# My Skill

Instructions for Claude when this skill is invoked.

## When to Use

- When the user asks about...
- When working with...

## How to Execute

1. First...
2. Then...
validation_name_max_length: Name must be {max} characters or less (currently {current})
validation_name_pattern: Name must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
validation_name_no_xml: Name cannot contain XML tags (< or >)
validation_description_max_length: Description must be {max} characters or less (currently {current})
validation_description_no_xml: Description cannot contain XML tags (< or >)
validation_content_lines_warning: Content has {count} lines, exceeding the recommended {max} lines.
validation_content_lines_warning_split: Content has {count} lines, exceeding the recommended {max} lines. Consider splitting into separate reference files.
command_import_markdown_hint: Paste or import a .md file with YAML frontmatter
command_name_rules: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only (max {max} chars)
command_allowed_tools_hint: Comma-separated list of tools. Use * for all tools. Supports patterns like Bash(git:*)
command_argument_hint_desc: Shows user expected arguments format. Use $ARGUMENTS in content to receive them.
command_model_hint: Optionally force a specific model when executing this command
skill_import_markdown_hint: Paste or import a .md file with YAML frontmatter
skill_name_rules: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
skill_description_hint: Claude uses this to decide when to invoke the skill (max {max} chars)
skill_allowed_tools_hint: Comma-separated list of tools. Use * for all tools. Supports patterns like Bash(git:*)
skill_model_hint: Optionally force a specific model when executing this skill
skill_disable_model_invocation_hint: Prevent Claude from automatically invoking this skill. Useful for skills that should only be called by other skills.
skill_content_hint: Instructions that tell Claude how to execute this skill (recommended: under {max} lines)
skill_file_confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this file?
empty_add_first_command: Add your first slash command to get started
empty_add_first_skill: Add your first agent skill to get started
empty_add_files_hint: Add references, assets, or scripts to enhance this skill
permission_mode_default_desc: Ask for approval on potentially dangerous operations
permission_mode_allow_edits_desc: Allow file edits without asking, but still ask for commands
permission_merged_view_desc: Combined view of all permission rules across all scopes. Rules are evaluated in order: deny first, then ask, then allow.
permission_specifier_hint: Use * for wildcard matching. Leave empty to match all uses of this tool.

memory_scope_project_desc: CLAUDE.md — project instructions shared with team via git
settings_editor_sync_enabled_editors_des: Enable the coding assistants you want to sync with. When enabled, skills, commands, sub-agents, and MCPs will be synced to all enabled editors simultaneously.
settings_editor_sync_no_editors_enabled: No editors enabled. Enable at least one editor to sync your configurations.
settings_editor_sync_github_token_desc_b: Add a personal access token to increase the GitHub API rate limit from 60 to 5,000 requests per hour.
settings_editor_sync_github_token_desc_a: No scopes are needed for public repositories.
settings_editor_sync_mcp_server_desc: Expose this app as an MCP server for programmatic control
settings_editor_sync_mcp_server_disabled: MCP server is disabled. Enable it to expose this app for programmatic control.
settings_editor_sync_stop_server_to_chan: Stop the server to change port
settings_editor_sync_available_tools_des: The MCP server exposes tools for managing:
settings_editor_sync_gateway_desc: Aggregate multiple MCPs into a single endpoint for Claude
settings_editor_sync_gateway_disabled_de: Gateway is disabled. Enable it to aggregate MCPs into a single endpoint.
settings_editor_sync_stop_gateway_to_cha: Stop the gateway to change port
settings_editor_sync_no_mcps_connected: No MCPs connected. Add MCPs to the gateway from the Library page.
settings_editor_sync_gateway_help: The gateway aggregates tools from multiple MCPs into a single endpoint. Add MCPs to the gateway from the Library page, then use the gateway URL in your Claude config instead of individual MCPs. Tool names are prefixed with their source MCP name (e.g., {example}).
settings_editor_sync_backup_desc: Create a backup of your MCP configurations before making changes.
analytics_generate_usage_hint: Use Claude Code to generate usage data, then refresh this page.
comparison_select_minimum: Select at least 2 projects to compare
comparison_select_range_hint: Choose 2-5 projects above to see side-by-side metrics
claude_json_empty_desc: MCPs configured through Claude Code will appear here
empty_add_first_hook: Add your first hook to get started
empty_add_first_subagent: Add your first sub-agent to get started
empty_create_hooks_assign: Create hooks and assign them to projects
empty_no_statuslines_match: No status lines match your search
empty_statusline_hint: Add segments to build your status line
label_tristate_cycle_hint: Click to cycle between inherit, enabled, and disabled
hook_event_notification_desc: Runs when Claude Code sends notifications
hook_event_permission_request_desc: Runs when a permission dialog is shown. Can allow/deny on behalf of user.
hook_event_post_tool_use_desc: Runs after a tool completes successfully. Can provide feedback to Claude.
hook_event_post_tool_use_matcher_hint: Tool name pattern (e.g., Bash, Write|Edit, mcp__server__tool)
hook_event_pre_compact_desc: Runs before a compact operation. Informational only, cannot block.
hook_event_pre_tool_use_desc: Runs before a tool is executed. Can block or modify tool input.
hook_event_pre_tool_use_matcher_hint: Tool name pattern (e.g., Bash, Write|Edit, mcp__server__tool)
hook_event_session_end_desc: Runs when the session ends. Cannot block, for cleanup tasks only.
hook_event_session_start_desc: Runs when a new Claude Code session starts or resumes
hook_event_session_start_matcher_hint: Source type: startup, resume, clear, compact
hook_event_stop_desc: Runs when the main agent finishes responding. Can prevent stopping.
hook_event_subagent_stop_desc: Runs when a subagent task finishes. Can prevent stopping.
hook_event_user_prompt_submit_desc: Runs when the user submits a prompt, before Claude processes it
mcp_empty_library_hint: Add an MCP server to get started
model_option_sonnet_desc: Best balance of speed and intelligence
model_option_opus_desc: Most capable model for complex tasks

settings_attribution_desc: Customize the attribution text shown in commits and PRs. Leave it empty to hide the footer, or unset it to use the default.
settings_auth_api_key_desc: Script that returns the API key used for authentication.
settings_auth_aws_export_desc: Script that exports AWS credentials into the environment.
settings_auth_aws_refresh_desc: Script that refreshes AWS authentication before requests.
settings_auth_desc: Configure helper scripts for API keys, OTEL headers, and AWS authentication.
settings_auth_otel_desc: Script that returns OTEL headers for requests.
settings_env_key_validation: Environment variable keys must be uppercase and use underscores only
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY: API key specifically for Claude Code
env_var_desc_ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL: Model for fast or low-cost operations
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSE: Disable telemetry and non-essential API calls
env_var_desc_NO_PROXY: Comma-separated list of hosts to bypass proxy
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK: Use AWS Bedrock as the provider (set to 1 to enable)
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX: Use Google Vertex AI as the provider (set to 1 to enable)
env_var_desc_CLOUD_ML_REGION: Google Cloud region for Vertex AI
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_SANDBOX_ENABLED: Enable sandbox mode (set to 1 to enable)
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: Override git author name for commits
env_var_desc_CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_AUTHOR_EMAI: Override git author email for commits
settings_file_suggestion_command_desc: Command to run when generating file suggestions.
settings_file_suggestion_desc: Configure how file suggestions are generated.
settings_file_suggestion_save_failed: Failed to save file suggestion settings
settings_marketplace_desc: Add and manage extra marketplace sources.
settings_mcp_approval_desc: Control which project-level MCP servers are automatically approved
settings_mcp_approval_disabled_desc: Disable automatic approval so every server requires manual confirmation
settings_mcp_approval_enable_all_desc: Automatically approve all project-level MCP servers
settings_mcp_approval_enabled_desc: Automatically approve only the MCP servers listed below
settings_model_available_desc: Choose which model shortcuts are available in the interface
settings_plugins_desc: Manage enabled plugins and tool restrictions.
settings_plugins_tools_hint: Enter a comma-separated list of allowed tools.
settings_sandbox_allow_unsandboxed_desc: Allow commands to run outside the sandbox when needed.
settings_sandbox_auto_allow_desc: Automatically allow Bash commands when sandboxing is enabled.
settings_sandbox_enabled_desc: Run commands in a sandboxed environment when supported.
settings_sandbox_excluded_desc: Commands listed here will bypass sandbox restrictions.
settings_sandbox_general_desc: Configure execution restrictions for sandboxed commands.
settings_sandbox_net_all_sockets_note: Ignored while all Unix sockets are allowed.
settings_sandbox_net_all_unix_desc: Allow access to any Unix socket path.
settings_sandbox_net_domains_desc: Allow outbound access to these domains.
settings_sandbox_net_local_binding_desc: Allow binding to local network interfaces.
settings_sandbox_net_unix_sockets_desc: Allow access to specific Unix socket paths.
settings_sandbox_network_desc: Configure network access available inside the sandbox.
settings_sandbox_weaker_nested_desc: Use a weaker sandbox policy when running inside a nested sandbox environment.
settings_session_cleanup_desc: Configure how long completed sessions are kept before cleanup
settings_session_desc: Manage cleanup, update channels, teammate mode, and plan storage
settings_session_plans_desc: Choose where plan files should be stored
settings_session_teammate_desc: Choose how teammate mode should run
statusline_gallery_desc: Pre-made status lines you can install with one click
statusline_gallery_refresh_hint: Try refreshing or check your gallery URL in settings
sessions_create_data_hint: Use Claude Code to create sessions, then refresh this page.
sessions_generate_data_hint: Use Claude Code to generate session data, then refresh this page.

managed_deployed_desc: Managed settings are deployed by IT administrators to enforce organization-wide policies. They are read-only and cannot be modified by users.
managed_field_allow_managed_hooks_only_d: Only allow hooks defined in managed settings or the SDK.
managed_field_allow_managed_permission_r: Only allow permission rules defined in managed settings.
managed_field_disable_bypass_permissions: Prevent users from bypassing the permission system.
managed_field_allowed_mcp_servers_desc: Allowlist of MCP servers that can be used.
managed_field_denied_mcp_servers_desc: Denylist of MCP servers that cannot be used.
managed_field_company_announcements_desc: Messages displayed to users at startup.
managed_field_force_login_method_desc: Force a specific login method, such as claudeai or console.
managed_field_force_login_org_uuid_desc: Auto-select a specific organization by UUID.
managed_only_desc: These fields can only be set via the managed settings file. They enforce enterprise policies and cannot be overridden.
managed_standard_desc: These regular settings are being enforced through the managed configuration file.

marketplace_env_configure_after_import: You'll need to configure these after import
